subject: Joint Distributions
question: (Order statistics) Let $X$ and $Y$ be two independent random variables with $X$ having a normal distribution with mean $\mu$ and variance 1 and $Y$ being the standard normal distribution. (a) Find the density of $Z = \min\{X, Y\}$. (b) For each $t \in \mathbb{R}$ calculate $\Pr(\max(X,Y) - \min(X,Y) > t)$.
explanation: (a) Fix a real number $z$. Then $F_Z(z) = \Pr(Z \le z) = 1 - \Pr(\min(X,Y) > z) = 1 - \Pr(X > z)\Pr(Y > z) = 1 - (1 - \Phi(z-\mu))(1 - \Phi(z))$. Hence, $f_Z(z) = (1 - \Phi(z-\mu))\varphi(z) + (1 - \Phi(z))\varphi(z-\mu)$ where $\varphi(z)$ is the pdf of the standard normal distribution. (b) If $t \le 0$ then $\Pr(\max(X,Y) - \min(X,Y) > t) = 1$. If $t > 0$ then $\Pr(\max(X,Y) - \min(X,Y) > t) = \Pr(|X - Y| > t) = 1 - \Phi\left(\frac{t-\mu}{\sqrt{2}}\right) + \Phi\left(\frac{-t-\mu}{\sqrt{2}}\right)$. Note that $X - Y$ is normal with mean $\mu$ and variance 2
